description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the node:carbon-alpine Docker image with a global before_script that runs "yarn install" and a cache keyed by $CI_JOB_ID for the node_modules directory. Define three stages: test, build, and publish. In the test stage add a test job that uses the geekykaran/headless-chrome-node-docker image, runs "yarn run test:ci", and stores the coverage directory as a 30‑minute artifact while extracting coverage with the regex /Functions\s+:\s(\d+(?:\.\d+)?%)/. In the build stage add a build job that runs "yarn run build" and saves the dist folder as a 30‑minute artifact, and a build:documentation job that runs only on tags matching the pattern ^v\d\.\d\.\d$, runs "yarn run compodoc", and saves the documentation folder as a 30‑minute artifact. In the publish stage add a pages job that depends on build, build:documentation, and test, runs "mkdir public" and "cp -r documentation/* public/", publishes the public directory as a pages artifact, and runs only on tags matching ^v\d\.\d\.\d$; also add a publish job that depends on build and build:documentation, runs "echo '//registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken=${NPM_TOKEN}' > .npmrc" and "npm run publish:lib", and runs only on the same tag pattern.
